Leon Uris

# Leon Uris

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Aug 03, 2024

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

3

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!vIec!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fcbe26dd6-90e1-4962-9970-056564325a94_679x382.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!vIec!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fcbe26dd6-90e1-4962-9970-056564325a94_679x382.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Leon Uris, one of my growing up memories, and one of the best when it came to historical fiction, especially Jewish history. In a sense it was his novels, that sparked my curiosity on Jewish history, as also World History.

He was born in Baltimore to a Polish immigrant and a first generation Russian American. Leon was 17 when he took part in WW2, as part of US Marine Corps, served in South Pacific, fought at Guadalcanal and Tarawa. After the war, he worked as a journalist, and then he wrote his first novel Battle Cry, based on his experiences in the Pacific campaign, about a battalion made up of people from diverse backgrounds, who bond together and their experiences at Tarawa, Guadalcanal and Saipan. It was made into a succesful movie too.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!C6zF!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7006a09e-7b60-4ecd-bfbc-82e5af2273b8_259x383.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!C6zF!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7006a09e-7b60-4ecd-bfbc-82e5af2273b8_259x383.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!F228!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F029d1fcc-30f0-4d3e-ac75-6c54ffe6ac70_448x680.jpeg)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!F228!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F029d1fcc-30f0-4d3e-ac75-6c54ffe6ac70_448x680.jpeg "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

His next novel The Angry Hills, was set in Greece, set against the backdrop of the German occupation during WW2, and the Greek resistance. A very good read, made into a succesful movie in 1959 starring Robert Mitchum.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!HGRk!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F80f60e80-5f75-4d1f-8d88-8727f76e5e3a_220x339.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!HGRk!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F80f60e80-5f75-4d1f-8d88-8727f76e5e3a_220x339.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!2mN8!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fabaae112-e9e2-4eb4-8549-8b4e0a2a5332_250x369.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!2mN8!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fabaae112-e9e2-4eb4-8549-8b4e0a2a5332_250x369.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

His finest work though would be Exodus, as he explores the birth of Israel, and takes us through Jewish history, the Holocaust, and First Arab Israeli War, truly epic read. And being a Jew himself, Uris gives an excellent insight into their history and culture. There was also a 1960 movie with Paul Newman in the lead.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!T-8M!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ff60391e0-c2a7-465f-a884-3717c6afea4f_260x382.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!T-8M!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ff60391e0-c2a7-465f-a884-3717c6afea4f_260x382.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!WVBs!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4715a934-dffb-4eeb-a39f-7b89f72b148b_360x235.jpeg)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!WVBs!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4715a934-dffb-4eeb-a39f-7b89f72b148b_360x235.jpeg "Image")

Exodus was one of the biggest bestsellers of it's time, and it's impact was massive among the Jewish diaspora. Especially among Jews in Russia, who were denied permission to emigrate, they circulated the Russian versions of the novel, passing from one generation to another.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!JhVE!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F5dfb300a-be2c-429d-9d8a-338722fd6652_254x393.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!JhVE!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F5dfb300a-be2c-429d-9d8a-338722fd6652_254x393.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!v-XK!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F45b5df53-6045-4c94-b8bd-8a39228e0d9e_252x393.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!v-XK!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F45b5df53-6045-4c94-b8bd-8a39228e0d9e_252x393.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Another brilliant novel Mila 18, about the Warsaw Ghetto Uprising against the Nazis. The title comes from the name of the undeground bunker, which was the HQ of Jewish resistance fighters. One brilliant read, that is both uplifting as well as depressing when the Jews are let down by collaborators among them.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!QCvp!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F74329596-0255-486f-ba10-d7bb2a74d798_257x386.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!QCvp!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F74329596-0255-486f-ba10-d7bb2a74d798_257x386.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Armageddo set in the backdrop of the Berlin Blockade, and the subsequent Airlift by the Allies, is another brilliant work. An excellent look at Cold War politics, the Allied occupation of Berlin after the War, splitting into 4 zones are wonderfully explored, as also the Airlift in very vivid detail.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ojGp!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F082303c4-bb57-4cb5-8dd7-43a8b6428308_164x254.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ojGp!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F082303c4-bb57-4cb5-8dd7-43a8b6428308_164x254.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Trinity is set in the backdrop of Irish history, covering events from the Great Famine of the 1840s to the Easter Rising in 1916, as also the Irish nationalist movement, as he explores it really well through the lives of 4 familes, 2 Catholic and 2 Protestant.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!WpTo!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fbffc5970-6842-4826-b09e-8a0f871bc886_220x331.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!WpTo!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fbffc5970-6842-4826-b09e-8a0f871bc886_220x331.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

One of his most epic novels ever QB VII, a 4 part courtroom drama, about an author Abraham Cady, who is on trial for libel, when he names an eminent surgeon Sir Adam Kelno, as a notorious concentration camp doctor.

The trial brings out many facets of the Holocaust, as well as "respectable" people who had been part of it. This is one of those novels, that needs a long detailed read. It was later adapted into an equally great TV series.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!T6OW!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F480db867-8c6d-445b-a2fd-8498abe6ce74_220x316.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!T6OW!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F480db867-8c6d-445b-a2fd-8498abe6ce74_220x316.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!WGXC!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F5009298e-ae84-4b48-96a5-5953422a6532_220x319.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!WGXC!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F5009298e-ae84-4b48-96a5-5953422a6532_220x319.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The Haj looks at the birth of Israel from the view point of a Palestinian Arab. The novel showcases how the Arabs often are their own worst enemies, including the lead character Ibrahim, the chieftain of a Palestinian village. Not surprisingly it was not liked by critics, but worth a read.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!p7-y!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4e27aa7b-a1e1-46fc-b7c7-cec9f39f0532_263x394.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!p7-y!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4e27aa7b-a1e1-46fc-b7c7-cec9f39f0532_263x394.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Mitla Pass was another good read, about an author Gideon Zadok, who follows an airborne team to secure the Mitla Pass during the Suez Crisis. It was based on the real Israeli operation that was headed by Ariel Sharon, who later became the 11th PM.  

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!GQ1v!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F044bb135-92e7-4619-a29e-c5b5770345f9_233x353.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!GQ1v!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F044bb135-92e7-4619-a29e-c5b5770345f9_233x353.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Apart from his novels, Uris also wrote the screenplay for the Western Gunfight at Ok Corral, apart from the screenplay for his own novel Battle Cry.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ODAc!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fbf7be669-6680-437a-b46d-d77a3d7dd232_220x428.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ODAc!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fbf7be669-6680-437a-b46d-d77a3d7dd232_220x428.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Leon Uris has been one of growing up memories, his historical fiction novels remain an excellent read even now. Especially if you are looking to know more on Jewish history. His Exodus is a must read for anyone wanting to know the backdrop of Israel and how it was formed.

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

3

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
